body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Internet Research Task Force Plenary Report
IAB Report to the Community for IETF 116
IETF Chair and IESG Report, IETF 116

knowledge of of what what's going on and the main thing that's going on at this very moment is cherry blossom uh it's it's very big um uh I spent last week in Hokkaido where there was no Blossom no leaves uh just snow but as we came South and South and South so the uh the blossom was was coming out um another little essential for this story is to know that uh in Japanese culture the uh Carissa um the the crow or Raven is uh it symbolizes love and and gratitude uh and good luck um and in fact in the the ancient Japanese Shinto religion uh the Ravens were believed to be messengers of the Gods um and you see them if you if you look at classical Japanese art you see pictures of of Ravens or crows uh going back a long long way so I I took those two things and um uh Blended them with a very old um a western uh fairy tale or folk story called Snow White and blood red um it's it's the Forerunner of the well-known uh Snow White tale that we we have from the Grimm Brothers and that makes its way into Disney um in the the Grimm Brothers version the queen uh is sewing um at her window and she pricks her finger with the needle and uh three drops of blood fall uh onto the snow and she says how I wish I had a daughter that had skin as white as the snow lips as red as blood and hair as black as ebony I'm not sure where the ebony came from we've got the snow and the blood but suddenly hair is black as ebony anyway um that leads on to the whole Snow White uh story so um we have snow white and cherry blossom pink there was once a princess in the Kanagawa area of Japan she lived in a beautiful Tower of wood that was carved and painted with the most delicate colors on The Ridges of the red-tiled roof stood dragons and ogres whose job it was to ward off the evil spirits and keep the princess safe in the Walled Garden there were many useful herbs as well as flowering plants and of course an old and much loved cherry tree the princess wanted for nothing she had everything that her loving husband could provide for her and the people of the surrounding Countryside were always passing by with gifts of fruit or songbirds for the princess was much loved for her grace and beauty as well as for her kindness and happy smile except the princess dearly wanted a child a sun would have been nice but a little daughter was what the princess dreamed of but it seemed wrong to her to have so many other lovely things and still want for something else so the princess tried to put a sadness behind her and carry on with life as though everything was perfect only sometimes would she grow wistful and stand at her window in the high tower looking out at the distant sea or at the far off Hills and sighing for the child she did not have one Springtime the cherry tree put on a show of Blossom more spectacular than anyone could remember the clumps of pink flowers tinged with red and white stood out against the Clear Blue Sky the princess delighting in the bright colors asked for a few small branches to be brought to her room and there they enhance the decorations bringing a smile to everyone's face foreign but it was a strange year almost as soon as the old tree had flowered there was the heaviest of frosts the cold weather cut through everything setting ice on the horse's drinking trough and making the servants rush to light the black iron stoves in the rooms in the tower the wind blew sharp and spiteful out of the North and the tree gave up its blossoms to the Gale that night the snow started to fall and by the next morning deep drifts had built up in the garden and a blanket of white covered ever covered everything the princess stood at her window and felt her old sadness she held a small sprig of cherry blossom preserved from the frost by the heat of her room and gazed out at the whiteness she started to dream again of the child she didn't have as she stood there a large glossy black bird landed on the windowsill and picked as if asking to be let in it was a carousel a raven driven in from in front of the Wind from its normal home in the hills and searching for food the princess thought what I wouldn't give for a daughter who had hair as black and shiny as the Raven's feathers she could have skin as white as the snow and lips as Pink as cherry blossom how I would love her and cherish her and maybe the princess set her thoughts out loud for the Raven picked again and called out let me in and give me food and I will grant your wish the princess immediately rushed to find bread and meat for the bird she set them on a gold plate on the inlaid table in her dressing room she poured fresh water into a delicate porcelain dish and served it to the bird who ate and drank greedily when is it finished the bird's side cleaned its beak on the tablecloth and gave the princess some instructions take a handful of petals from the cherry blossom and crush them in your fist then throw them out into the wind and snow the princess did as she was told squeezing the flowers and releasing the pretty smell of spring before sending the rosy pink remains out wherever the icy blast would carry them now take one of my wing feathers The Raven told her and place it under your pillow when you sleep tonight you'll be surprised before you know it you'll have your child and once the princess had the feather the blue the bird flew off into the night of course the princess was a little skeptical this is this did not seem like an a likely way of getting a daughter but she was so very desperate she would try anything and that night she slept with the feather under her pillow only a few later it was obvious to everyone that the princess was expecting the baby there was much joy and celebration in the tower and in the surrounding Countryside everyone shared the princess's happiness eventually when the time was right princess laid a perfect Snow White egg from which hatched a raven chick with feathers as black and glossy as night and I is the living the livid pink of cherry blossom that's that's all you get [Applause] and that that basically brings us to an end uh thanking again our three students um uh jprs for the food which was which was wonderfully generous and I'd also like to thank the Secretariat for um jumping through hoops to to make this a illegal side meeting instead of uh um something else like this and and if you like the idea of stories and telling stories and and sharing um food and being together then come and talk to us come and join our mailing list and and perhaps we'll do something again at a future ietf thank you all arigato [Applause] thank you Adrian this is wonderful uh and please take your rubbish to the back thank you
